name: fNIRS Hyperscanning — Social Touch & Inter-Brain Synchrony
description: An open fNIRS hyperscanning dataset of 47 female dyads (24 friend pairs, 23 stranger pairs) measuring concurrent hemodynamic activity in both partners during social touch, with trial-by-trial valence/arousal ratings and extensive questionnaires. It enables study of inter-brain synchrony, social relationship, and affective touch — a model two-brain dataset for real-world social neuroscience.
citation: An fNIRS hyperscanning dataset on the modulation of synchrony by social relationship during social touch (2025). Scientific Data, 12.

publications.title: fNIRS Hyperscanning: A door to real-world social neuroscience research
